answer:
**The Pārāśara Smṛti is presented as the authoritative *dharma* specifically for the Kali *yuga*.**[^1][^2]

Its opening frames the entire text as detailing the *dharma* beneficial for the present Kali age, including rules of purity and conduct.[^3] It explicitly states that Parāśara's *dharmas* are to be followed in the Kali *yuga*, succeeding those of Śaṅkha and Likhita from the Dvāpara.[^4]

The core duties of Brāhmaṇas are the famous **six daily acts (*ṣaṭkarma*)**, which a Brāhmaṇa devoted to them—along with worship of deities and guests, and eating remnants of fire offerings—does not perish.[^5] These are enumerated as:

> Twilight prayers and bathing, recitation of sacred texts (*japa*), fire offerings (*homa*), worship of deities and guests, hospitality to an *atithi* (a guest, who must be honored), and the *Vaiśvadeva* (the daily ritual offering to all deities) — these are the six duties to be performed day by day.[^6]

Chapter 2 further specifies that a householder Brāhmaṇa in the Kali *yuga* may engage in agriculture (with caveats on treatment of oxen and sharing produce) *while* performing these six duties, plus feeding learned Brāhmaṇas, the five great sacrifices, and Vedic study.[^7]

No other chapters contain more specific enumerations of Brāhmaṇa duties tied explicitly to the Kali age; the text's structure positions these as the foundational practices for *dvijas* (especially Brāhmaṇas) in this era.[^8]

[^1]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1 (/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-121575).

[^2]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1 (/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-d9b4b0).

[^3]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1, [p-22e02e](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-22e02e).

[^4]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1, [p-121575](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-121575).

[^5]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1, [p-f4eb93](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-f4eb93). Par_1.38.

[^6]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1, [p-40daa2](/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/#p-40daa2). Par_1.39.

[^7]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.2 (/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/02/translation/#p-d394a0).

[^8]: *Pārāśara Smṛti*, ch.1 (/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/01/translation/). The chapter wiki cards and searches confirm no dedicated later section on Kali-specific Brāhmaṇa duties beyond these.
